title: eremophila neglecta
intro: Eremophila neglecta, commonly known as the desert emu bush, is a small, shrubby plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Eremophila neglecta include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Eremophila neglecta can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
